candidates.title: A Study in India on the Immune Response and Safety of a Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Older Adults (OA) Vaccine When Given to Older Adults 60 Years of Age and Above and Adults 50-59 Years of Age at Increased Risk (AIR) of Respiratory Syncytial Virus Lower Respiratory Tract Disease (RSV-LRTD)
candidates.study_design: ClinicalTrials.gov study type: INTERVENTIONAL; phase: PHASE3; allocation: RANDOMIZED
candidates.outcome_domain: RSV prevention, immunization, protection, safety, policy, or implementation signal
candidates.key_finding: The purpose of the study is to evaluate the immunogenicity and safety of a single dose of investigational RSVPreF3 OA vaccine in Indian older adults 60 years of age (YOA) and above and Indian adults 50-59 YOA at increased risk of RSV-LRTD.
candidates.evidence_signal: Automated ClinicalTrials.gov inclusion; not manually verified.

candidates.title: Epidemiological Characteristics and Clinical Burden of RSV Infection in Children Under 24 Months in Ho Chi Minh City (2026-2028)
candidates.outcome_domain: RSV prevention, immunization, protection, safety, policy, or implementation signal
candidates.key_finding: This is a prospective, observational, multicenter study employing continuous, convenience sampling, stratified by study site and by fortnightly interval, enrolling until the target sample sizes are achieved over the first epidemiological season (2026) and the second epidemiological season (2027). The study population includes children aged 0 to 24 months presenting to the outpatient or inpatient facilities of the study sites with signs and symptoms of acute respiratory infection (ARI). candidates.title: RSV Immunisation Status in Queensland (Australia)
candidates.outcome_domain: RSV prevention, immunization, protection, safety, policy, or implementation signal
candidates.key_finding:
The primary objective of this study is to determine the nirsevimab immunisation rate in eligible infants (according to Queensland Paediatric Respiratory Syncytial Virus Prevention Program recommendation) in Queensland, Australia.

The study will focus on:

1\. Assessing the immunisation rates among eligible infants (born from 1 February 2024 to 15 April 2025) in their first Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV)-season in Queensland.

Secondary objectives of this study are as follows:

1. To analyse reasons of parents to decide for or against immunisation of their infant with nirsevimab.
